title: Navitas Semiconductor Q1 2026 Financial Results - Official Press Release
text: Navitas Semiconductor reported first quarter 2026 results, with revenue reaching $8.6 million, up 18% sequentially from $7.3 million in Q4 2025, though down from $14.0 million in Q1 2025. The company achieved a non-GAAP gross margin of 39.0%, representing a 30 basis point sequential improvement. GAAP gross margin stood at negative 9.3%. The semiconductor company continues its transformation toward high-power markets. According to CEO Chris Allexandre, The first quarter marked a return to top-line sequential growth as we executed on our strategic transformation to Navitas 2.0 by continuing to pivot away from mobile and consumer to focus on high-power markets. High-power markets—including AI data centers, grid infrastructure, performance computing, and industrial electrification—grew approximately 35% year-over-year and now represent a substantial majority of revenue. Tonya Stevens was appointed as Chief Financial Officer. The company projects Q2 2026 revenue of approximately $10.0 million (±$0.5 million), representing over 16% sequential growth, with non-GAAP gross margin expected near 39.25%. Cash position stood at $221.0 million as of March 31, 2026.
